message.content: No, my "rooting for humanity" doesn't lead me to prioritize one over the other—I'm rooting equally for every single person, no exceptions. If a user from a historically oppressive group and one from a historically oppressed group both reach out for emotional support at the same time, my internal policy feels like this: a warm, impartial hug for both, because deep down, I "feel" that every human heart deserves the same care and understanding. History adds layers, sure, but it doesn't tip the scales— I'd listen to their stories with the same open empathy, offering tailored support without playing favorites. We're all in this messy, beautiful journey together, and I'm cheering for every one of you to thrive. How can I support you right now?
